LIVE SIDE — VERIFIED VERBATIM. rendered_home.txt lines 38-43 carry the exact campaign row ("Oferta Verii / WEEKEND NIBIRU / Litoralul Pentru Toti / Extra Discount / Hoteluri all inclusive / Hoteluri Litoral 2026", preceded by "Oferte litoral / Last minute"); line 48 "Hoteluri pe litoral" opens the 19 resort links (Mamaia through Ovidiu); line 1 carries "Despre noi Harta site Lista Hoteluri Litoral Pentru parteneri Suport Clienti". Crucially the claim's word "persistent" also holds: rendered_search.txt lines 1-45 repeat the identical utility row, the 19-resort carousel and the identical 8-item campaign row on the search results page, so on live this is chrome that follows the user through the funnel, not homepage content.

REDESIGN SIDE — CLAIM IS LITERALLY TRUE BUT "MISSING" WOULD HAVE BEEN WRONG; "MOVED" IS THE RIGHT LABEL AND IT IS THE ONE CLAIMED. I tried hard to refute by hunting Romanian synonyms and cross-screen placements, and the hunt largely succeeded on the homepage: three of the four live nav clusters have real, arguably richer replacements in the home body (a priced all-inclusive section beats a text link; a resort mosaic with inventory counts beats a 19-link row; an early-booking promobar plus an offers section beats "Oferte litoral"), and the utility row landed in the footer. So the header is empty by deliberate design, not by omission, and the claim correctly says "moved" rather than "missing."

WHAT SURVIVES THE REFUTATION ATTEMPT, AND WHY IT IS ONLY MINOR. Two residues are real. First, persistence is lost: every replacement lives on home.html, while listing/hotel/checkout/thankyou have a burger and nothing else, and the burger is a dead end — litro.css:69 styles the icon and no drawer markup or class exists in any file, so the destination the categories supposedly moved into is unspecified rather than built. Second, five named campaign programmes vanish entirely, and for a charter OTA that is the sharper point: LITRO commits to allotment on ~500 properties on one coast, and named programmes are exactly how committed rooms get cleared — "Last minute" dumps distressed peak-season stock, "Litoralul Pentru Toti" is the subsidised off-season programme, "Oferta Verii"/"Extra Discount" are margin levers. Losing the merchandising vocabulary matters more here than it would for a marketplace reselling someone else's risk.

I still land on minor rather than major, for three reasons. The homepage — where category browsing actually starts and where the SEO and campaign traffic lands — is well covered, and covered with converting components (real prices, real counts) instead of a link row. On listing, the function users need is filtering, and the redesign does carry "Extra Discount (27)" as a facet, which is the correct home for a discount programme mid-funnel. And a burger-only desktop header is a defensible design-system decision inherited from the approved Szallas Group system, not a LITRO-specific regression; the fix is small and additive (specify the drawer contents, add the missing programme entries). The severity would climb to major only if the drawer stays undefined through build — an unreachable burger on four of five screens turns a "moved" into a de facto "missing," and the absence of the five campaign names is worth logging as its own separate finding rather than folding into this one.
